results.possibleIdentity: David Schoen was a software and telecommunications engineer active in New York City during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was a specialist in software telecommunications and systems analysis programming for Cybernetic Applications, Inc., a computer consulting and data processing firm located at 120 Liberty Street in New York. Schoen was a participant in Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a service organization founded by artists Robert Rauschenberg and Robert Whitman alongside Bell Labs engineers Billy Klüver and Fred Waldhauer to facilitate collaborations between the arts and the technical community. He was listed in the E.A.T. Technical Directory as an available technical consultant in the fields of computer technology and telecommunications, reflecting the organization's mission to match artists with experts in emerging technologies.
